summary: Reverted/reshaped recent CI changes by separating GitHub Pages publishing back out of the Melee build workflow. The old combined `.github/workflows/build.yml` became `.github/workflows/build-melee.yml` with Pages permissions and `gen-pages`/`deploy-pages` jobs removed, while a standalone `.github/workflows/publish-pages.yml` was added to generate and deploy pages using the existing `gen-pages:latest` container. A comment was added in the page generator entrypoint noting a race condition between `build-melee` and `gen-pages`. No PR discussion or review comments were present in the provided slice.
what_changed: Renamed `.github/workflows/build.yml` to `.github/workflows/build-melee.yml`, changing the workflow name from `build` to `build-melee` and run-name from `Build Melee & publish GitHub Pages` to `Build Melee`.
Removed GitHub Pages-specific permissions (`pages: write`, `id-token: write`) from the Melee build workflow, leaving it focused on build jobs.
Removed the `gen-pages` and `deploy-pages` jobs from `build-melee.yml`; these had generated pages after `build-ninja` and deployed them from the same workflow.
Added a new standalone `.github/workflows/publish-pages.yml` workflow with its own Pages permissions, `pages` concurrency group, `gen-pages` job, and `deploy-pages` job.

The new `publish-pages` workflow checks out the repository, logs into `ghcr.io`, pulls `doldecomp/melee/gen-pages:latest`, runs it with `/input` mounted read-only and `/output` mounted writable, uploads the output as a Pages artifact, and deploys only on the repository default branch.

Added a source comment in `.github/packages/gen-pages/entrypoint.sh`: `BUG race condition between build-melee and gen-pages`, immediately before fetching progress data from `https://progress.decomp.club/data/melee/GALE01?format=json&mode=all`.
smart_moves: Separated build validation from Pages publishing, reducing coupling between ordinary Melee build CI and documentation/progress page deployment.
Kept GitHub Pages elevated permissions scoped to the publishing workflow rather than the build workflow.
Used a dedicated `pages` concurrency group with `cancel-in-progress: false`, preserving serialized Pages deployments.
Preserved default-branch-only deployment via `if: github.ref_name == github.event.repository.default_branch`, while still allowing generation checks on pull requests.
Documented the known race condition near the progress data fetch rather than leaving the CI hazard implicit.
decomp_lessons: Keep decompilation build CI narrowly focused on matching/build validation; publishing status pages can be isolated into a separate workflow to avoid unnecessary permissions and job dependencies.
When progress pages depend on externally generated or asynchronously updated progress data, record race conditions explicitly so future CI changes do not assume deterministic ordering.
Containerized helper workflows should keep repository input read-only and write generated artifacts to a separate output mount, as shown by the `docker run` invocation in the Pages generator job.
Use workflow names that describe one responsibility, such as `build-melee` and `publish-pages`, instead of a combined build-and-publish workflow name.
naming_conventions: Workflow file names use explicit kebab-case purpose names: `build-melee.yml` and `publish-pages.yml`.
Workflow `name` values mirror the file purpose: `build-melee` and `publish-pages`.
Job names remain user-facing and descriptive: `Generate pages`, `Deploy to GitHub Pages`, `Make`.
The container image suffix for page generation is `gen-pages:latest`, combined with `IMAGE_REPO: doldecomp/melee`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The only explicit rationale-like note in the diff is the added `BUG race condition between build-melee and gen-pages` comment in `entrypoint.sh`; no reviewer explanation is available.
follow_up_queries: Why was the combined build-and-pages workflow reverted or split in PR 1269?
Investigate the documented race condition between `build-melee` and `gen-pages` around progress data fetching.
Check adjacent CI PRs before/after PR 1269 for the original CI change being reverted.
Verify whether `publish-pages` running on pull requests without deployment is still desired for current CI cost and permissions.
